name: The Coin-Keeper's Provisions
description: A squat stone building on the market square with a hand-painted sign of a coin being squeezed until it bleeds. Inside, every inch of shelf space is utilized. Rope, lanterns, rations, cookware, nails, canvas, ink, waterskins — if Millhaven needs it, Krassus stocks it, and Krassus has priced it to his advantage. The counter is caged with iron bars that Krassus installed himself. He watches customers from behind it with the unblinking focus of a predator monitoring prey. Despite the markup, the shop is indispensable — the nearest alternative is two days' travel. Tomás Aventide rents a corner stall where his latest venture unfolds and inevitably collapses.

sounds: The click of Krassus's counting beads
Tomás pitching his latest venture to anyone within earshot
Krassus's claws tapping the counter when he's calculating
The bell above the door — installed so Krassus can track traffic
smells: Whatever Tomás is selling this week
Old wood and dust from the back storeroom
Krassus's clam chowder — he eats lunch at the counter and never shares
atmosphere.lighting: Minimal. Krassus doesn't waste candles on ambiance. A skylight provides most illumination. The back corners are dim, which Krassus claims deters theft.

layout.name: The Shop Floor
layout.description: Narrow aisles between floor-to-ceiling shelves. Everything has a handwritten price tag in Krassus's cramped script. Common items near the front, expensive items behind the cage.

layout.name: The Cage
layout.description: Krassus's counter, enclosed in iron bars with a small transaction window. He conducts all business from in here. The lockbox is under the counter; the trap is above the lockbox.

layout.name: Tomás's Corner
layout.description: A rented stall near the entrance where Tomás Aventide displays his current enterprise. Last month: enchanted walking sticks. This month: 'essence of vigor' tonic. The display changes weekly.

layout.name: The Storeroom
layout.description: Behind a locked door. Krassus's surplus, bulk goods, and the items he bought cheap and is holding until prices rise. He keeps the key on a chain around his neck.

hooks: Krassus has been buying a suspicious amount of rope and chain — more than Millhaven could need
Tomás's latest tonic is actually working, which terrifies him because he has no idea what's in it
Georvin does odd jobs for Krassus in exchange for store credit and has started noticing inventory discrepancies
A merchant from upriver offered to open a competing store — Krassus's reaction was disproportionate

worldContext.location: The Coin-Keeper's Provisions, Market Square, Millhaven
